channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Sweden Arrests Chinese Captain of Russia-Linked Oil Tanker
item.description: Swedish authorities arrested the Chinese captain of a seized oil tanker with suspected Russian ties on charges of carrying false documents and violating seaworthiness laws, highlighting continued sanctions enforcement.

item.title: Russia Intensifies Drone Attacks on Ukrainian Port Infrastructure
item.description: Kyiv reports Russia has used over 800 drones against Ukrainian ports in the first four months of 2026—a tenfold increase from the same period last year—escalating attacks on critical export infrastructure.

item.title: Russia Hits Five Naftogaz Facilities as Energy Infrastructure Targeted
item.description: Russian attacks struck five facilities of Ukraine's state energy company Naftogaz, damaging equipment and causing fires, the CEO reported, continuing Moscow's campaign against Ukrainian energy assets.

item.title: UAE Energy Minister: OPEC Exit Not Directed at Anyone
item.description: Following its departure from OPEC, the UAE energy minister said the country owes it to investment partners to produce without restrictions while cooperating with other crude producers.

item.title: US to Raise EU Car Tariffs to 25%
item.description: The US Trade Representative confirmed Washington will proceed with President Trump's plan to increase EU car import tariffs to 25%, dealing a blow to German automakers already under pressure.

item.title: UAE Buys 10 Brazilian Military Aircraft as BRICS Defense Ties Deepen
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has purchased 10 Embraer C-390 military transport aircraft from Brazil, strengthening defense cooperation between two key BRICS+ members.

item.title: Senator Graham Demands Forceful US Response After Iran Targets UAE
item.description: Senator Lindsey Graham called for a decisive US military response after Iran launched missile and drone attacks against the UAE, warning that American credibility is on the line.

item.title: US destroys Iranian boats, intercepts missiles and drones in Strait of Hormuz operation
item.description: The US military destroyed six Iranian small boats and intercepted missiles and drones as it launched "Project Freedom" to reopen shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump launches Project Freedom to escort ships through Hormuz as Iran warns of ceasefire breach
item.description: President Trump announced a US-led effort to guide stranded ships through the Iran-controlled Strait of Hormuz, while Tehran warned the operation violates the fragile ceasefire.

item.title: Two killed as car rams crowd in Leipzig, Germany
item.description: A car rammed into a crowd in Leipzig, killing two people and injuring others. Authorities are investigating the incident.

item.title: Canada seeks closer ties with Europe as both face Trump pressure
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney is pursuing deeper cooperation with European allies as both face trade tensions and security concerns under the Trump administration.

item.title: Reuters analysis warns US inflation heading toward 4 percent
item.description: A Reuters analysis warns that US inflation is on track to reach 4%, challenging expectations of rate cuts as economic pressures mount.

item.title: Ukraine raids draft offices nationwide, uncovers $2M in corruption
item.description: Ukrainian police conducted nationwide raids on draft offices, uncovering corruption amounting to $2 million as authorities crack down on graft during wartime.

item.title: Macron doubts Trump Hormuz operation, says France will not participate
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron expressed doubts about Trump's Hormuz operation and confirmed France will not participate, highlighting growing US-European divergence.

item.title: North Korean football team to visit South Korea for first time in 8 years
item.description: A North Korean women's football team will visit South Korea for the first time in 8 years, a rare instance of sports diplomacy between the Koreas.

item.title: Iran war fuel shock undercuts value of Japan airline frequent flyer miles
item.description: Japanese airlines JAL and ANA are raising fuel surcharges due to Middle East tensions, reducing the effective value of frequent flyer miles for travelers.

item.title: Putin Declares May 8-9 Ceasefire in Ukraine for Victory Day
item.description: President Putin declared a two-day ceasefire in Ukraine for May 8-9 to mark Russia's World War II Victory Day, while Zelenskiy proposed his own earlier ceasefire.

item.title: UAE Condemns Iranian Attacks as 'Dangerous Escalation'
item.description: The UAE condemned Iranian missile and drone attacks as a dangerous escalation, as fires burned at oil facilities in Fujairah following the strikes.

item.title: Wall Street Falls, Oil Jumps 5% on Iran War Escalation
item.description: US stocks retreated from records while oil prices surged 5% as Middle East tensions intensified, with investors assessing the economic impact of the Iran conflict.

item.title: NATO Chief Says Europe Has Gotten Trump's Message on Defense
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said European allies have understood Trump's demands for increased defense spending, calling for urgent action.

item.title: Rubio to Visit Rome Amid Rising US Tensions with Pope Leo and Italy
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will visit Rome May 6-8 following tensions between the US and the Vatican over policy differences with Pope Leo XIV.

item.title: UK in Talks to Join EU 105 Billion Ukraine Loan for Defense Orders
item.description: The UK is entering talks to join the EU's 105 billion euro Ukraine loan mechanism, which would fund European defense orders supporting Kyiv.

item.title: Barclays Bets No Fed Rate Cuts in 2026 as Iran War Fuels Inflation
item.description: Barclays joined other brokerages predicting no Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026, citing inflationary pressures from the Iran conflict and elevated energy prices.

item.title: Dubai Faces Capital Flight as Iran War Tests Financial Hub Status
item.description: Dubai's role as a regional financial hub is under strain as capital flows out amid Iran conflict fears and regional instability.

item.title: China Surpasses Japan in Next-Gen Solar Cell Patent Filings
item.description: China has overtaken Japan in perovskite solar cell patent filings, signaling a shift in leadership for next-generation renewable energy technology.
